title: Scotland vs Haiti injury news: Steve Clarke mulls over major decision on trio as World Cup campaign begins
original_text:
Scotland vs Haiti injury news: Steve Clarke mulls over major decision on trio as World Cup campaign begins
Here is the latest team news ahead of Scotland v Haiti at the World Cup 2026 at the Gillette Stadium this weekend
Scotland will get their World Cup campaign underway this weekend as they take on Haiti in Group C at the Gillette Stadium in Foxborough on Sunday (kick-off: 2am).
Their pre-tournament preparations now done and dusted, Steve Clarke’s men head into the tournament on the back of two impressive victories, where they downed Curacao 4-1 before thrashing Bolivia 4-0 in New Jersey last weekend.
Set to face Morocco and Brazil in their other group fixtures, the national team are expected to be backed by hordes of Tartan Army supporters as they announce their arrival back on the world stage for the first time since 1998.
Their opponents, Haiti, are making history of their own too, with the Caribbean nation taking part in just their second World Cup, having only previously qualified in 1974.
Ahead of the game at the Gillette Stadium, The Scotsman has all the latest team news:
1. Craig Gordon - Scotland - RETURN TO STARTING XI?
Will it be Gordon, or will it be Angus Gunn? The decision on who to start in goal for Scotland is a major one, and Steve Clarke has a tough decision ahead. | Getty Images
2. Scott McKenna - Scotland - MISSED TRAINING
The big Dinamo Zagreb centre-back was absent from training this week, but his issue is not considered serious and he should be available for selection. | Getty Images Photo: Getty Images
3. Derrick Etienne - Haiti - RETURN TO STARTING XI?
The Toronto FC winger was a late substitute in last weekend's final pre-World Cup warm up defeat against Peru, but is in contention to start for Haiti in Foxborough against Scotland. | AFP via Getty Images
4. Billy Gilmour - Scotland - OUT
Sadly ruled out of the tournament after suffering a knee injury in the pre-World Cup friendly vs Curacao, the Napoli midfielder hasn't travelled to the United States and has been replaced by Tyler Fletcher in the squad.
